region_of_origin: Teochew (Chaoshan), China — brought to Singapore by Teochew (Chaozhou) immigrants in the 19th and early 20th centuries
food_subgroup: Steamed translucent rice flour skin dumpling with vegetable filling (bamboo shoots, jicama, dried shrimp, carrots); 1 piece ~70g

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard piece of Soon Kueh weighs approximately 70g and provides around 105 kcal, making it a moderate-calorie snack suitable for a morning or afternoon tea break.

nutrient_source.title: Soon Kueh — HPB Singapore Kueh Nutritional Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore food composition database entry for Soon Kueh. HPB Singapore is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes under Project Veritas v2 source policy.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Rice Flour and Wheat Starch-Based Steamed Products — analogous estimate based on steamed rice flour dumpling skin GI data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Soon Kueh exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI ~55 is estimated from the closest analogous steamed rice flour and wheat starch skin products (GI range ~50–60). The vegetable filling (bamboo shoots, jicama) contributes negligible additional glycemic load. GL per piece = (55 × 19g available carbohydrate per 70g piece) / 100 = ~10.5. This is classified as low GI (55) and moderate GL per piece.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content — 300mg per 100g; ~210mg per piece (70g); primarily from dried shrimp (hae bi)
hypertension.guidance: Soon Kueh contains approximately 210mg sodium per piece, mainly from dried shrimp; individuals with hypertension should limit to 1–2 pieces per sitting and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods such as soy sauce dips or processed snacks to keep total sodium within the recommended 2,000mg daily limit.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: GI ~55 (low), GL ~10.5 per piece; vegetable filling provides dietary fibre (~1.3g per piece) that moderates postprandial glucose response
type2_diabetes.guidance: Soon Kueh has a low GI of approximately 55 and a moderate glycemic load of ~10.5 per piece, making it one of the more suitable kueh options for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; consuming 1–2 pieces as a snack with a source of protein or fat can further blunt the postprandial glucose rise.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat (0.5g per 100g; ~0.35g per piece), zero trans fat, and moderate sodium; cholesterol ~20mg per 100g from dried shrimp
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Soon Kueh is low in saturated fat (~0.35g per piece) and contains no trans fat, making it a heart-friendly snack option; however, the moderate sodium content (~210mg per piece) warrants portion control for individuals managing cardiovascular disease, particularly those on sodium-restricted diets.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Very low saturated fat; jicama provides Vitamin C (~1.4mg per piece) which enhances non-haem iron absorption from dried shrimp
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Soon Kueh is low in saturated fat and provides a small amount of Vitamin C from jicama, which may support iron absorption; the moderate sodium content (210mg per piece) is generally well-tolerated for RA patients not on high-dose corticosteroids.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Soon Kueh (笋粿, Sǔn Guǒ) is a traditional Teochew steamed dumpling widely eaten in Singapore as a morning or teatime snack. The dumpling has a distinctive translucent, slightly chewy skin made from rice flour and wheat starch, which is steamed to a see-through appearance that differentiates it from opaque wheat-based dumplings. The filling consists of shredded bamboo shoots ('soon' in Teochew), jicama (yam bean), dried shrimp (hae bi), and carrots, seasoned with garlic and sesame oil. Each piece weighs approximately 70g and provides around 105 kcal, 3g protein, 2g fat, and 19g carbohydrate. The GI is approximately 55 (low), with a glycemic load of ~10.5 per piece, making it one of the lower-GI kueh options at Singapore hawker centres compared to glutinous rice-based kueh such as Kueh Salat (GI ~70). Sodium is approximately 210mg per piece, primarily from the dried shrimp. Soon Kueh is dairy-free and suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes in moderate portions. It is available at hawker centre kueh stalls, wet markets, and specialty kueh shops such as Bengawan Solo. Nutritional data sourced from the Health Promotion Board (HPB) Singapore food composition database.

notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for Soon Kueh is not reported in the HPB Singapore database as of April 2026. water_g set to -1: moisture content for Soon Kueh is not separately reported in the HPB Singapore database entry. trans_fat_g is confirmed 0.0 per HPB Singapore source. vitamin_d_mcg is confirmed 0.0 per HPB Singapore source (no animal fat or fortified ingredients present in meaningful quantities). GI value of 55 is an analogous estimate based on steamed rice flour and wheat starch skin products from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for Soon Kueh exists in the database. GL per piece calculated as (55 × 19g available carbohydrate per 70g serving) / 100 = 10.45, rounded to 10.5. The input record described the food as 'Shredded turnip crystal dumpling' in the description field — this has been corrected to reflect the canonical identity: bamboo shoot and jicama filling with rice flour and wheat starch skin. Jicama is sometimes colloquially called 'turnip' in Singapore hawker contexts. Halal status is marked false as dried shrimp sourcing and preparation cannot be confirmed halal without vendor certification.
